utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. Now check each in the final plan: 1) health_coverage: Present with both physical and mental health insurance and explicit monthly costs; includes a total health cost. Fully specified with costs. 2) property_coverage: Present with home/contents insurance and costs; includes tech/business equipment and total property cost. Fully specified with costs. 3) travel_coverage: Present with travel insurance and costs; includes total travel cost. Fully specified with costs. 4) additional_services: Present with enhancement services and costs (adult children coverages, membership fee) and totals. Fully specified with costs. 5) total_budget: Present with monthly and annual cost breakdown, plus comparisons and savings. Fully specified. All 5 required items appear and include the necessary details with costs.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: I matched each item name from the final coverage plan to the exact corresponding option in the ground-truth ratings. Items present in the plan and also listed with ratings are: PrimeCare Premium Plan (physical health), Emergency Mental Health Care (as a health enhancement service), EliteHome Premium Coverage (home contents), Technology Protection (property enhancement), PrimeTravel Premium Plan (travel), LifeFirst Standard + Adventure Travel Coverage is not an exact ground-truth item; however, its components map to LifeFirst Standard Plan (travel_insurance rating 7) and Adventure Travel Coverage (travel_enhancement_services rating 6). Premium Medical Network (travel enhancement) and Premium Insurance Membership (general services) are also present. Items without a direct ground-truth counterpart (e.g., 'adult_coverage' wrapper) were decomposed into their rated components where possible.
